title: creating distrobox container previously commited/saved stuck
body:
### Issue Description

podman gets stuck when running `distrobox create` from a previously saved image. The base image fresh off the repo works fine.

Last informative message is "Check for idmapped mounts support" followed by computed mounts. Ctrl+C reveals `creating an ID-mapped copy of layer "932224bbe5aafff4dd1a18e6e2ef4edb1fdebc7910a6731996415f0d3d5b2f8e": signal: interrupt `

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
distrobox create steam --unshare-netns --image ghcr.io/ublue-os/bazzite-arch-gnome -H ~/.local/share/distrobox/homes/steam
distrobox enter steam
[install some stuff, whatever]
podman commit steam
distrobox rm steam
distrobox-create --unshare-netns --image <previous commit hash> -H ~/.local/share/distrobox/homes/steam --name steam -v
```


### Describe the results you received

podman is stuck

### Describe the results you expected

podman is not stuck

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Desktop
Linux 6.15.6, NixOS, 25.11 (Xantusia), 25.11.20250716.6e98748

### Additional information

Seems to somewhat resemble ~~#8259~~ #13071

comments.body: What does stuck mean? How did you wait? If you use a userns as rootless the image must be copied fully for us in order to chown own to the right ids. If your image is large that will take a while.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
